mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of September 2008 reflects labor market stress
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative UNRATE changes do not produce significantly lower forward returns than positive changes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and increases borrowing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the tightening cycle persists.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales combined with falling housing starts indicates weakening consumer demand and construction activity, signaling recession risk that drives negative equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 965k units in July 2008 reflects housing market collapse
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not produce significantly lower forward returns than periods of single-series decline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month signals reduced manufacturing output and economic slack, indicating weakening corporate profitability and negative earnings revisions that compress S&P 500 valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not produce significantly lower 5-day forward returns than INDPRO increases (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declining month-over-month combined with rising unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market deterioration and loss of consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession expectations intensify.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50k month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,473k and UNRATE at 6.1% in August 2008
regime_hint: labor market recession and financial crisis
failure_condition: Joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE does not produce significantly lower forward returns than single-series deterioration (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly declines below 1.0% annualized rate, signaling near-zero or negative economic growth that triggers recession fears and equity risk premium expansion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,740.3 billion as of Q2 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPI-U rising above 3.0% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals stagflationary pressure that constrains monetary policy flexibility and increases real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 219.181 in July 2008 reflects elevated inflation
failure_condition: Joint condition of high inflation and elevated rates does not produce significantly lower forward returns than either condition alone (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining below 900k annualized units signals severe contraction in residential construction demand and forward-looking housing market weakness, indicating negative wealth effects and reduced consumer spending that depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 965k units in July 2008 approaching critical threshold
regime_hint: housing market collapse and wealth destruction
failure_condition: HOUST below 900k does not produce significantly lower 5-day forward returns than HOUST above 900k (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
